2604.14862.title: Schema Key Wording as an Instruction Channel in Structured Generation under Constrained Decoding
2604.14862.paper_summary: Constrained decoding has been widely adopted for structured generation with large language models (LLMs), ensuring that outputs satisfy predefined formats such as JSON and XML. However, existing approaches largely treat schemas as purely structural constraints and overlook the possibility that their linguistic formulation may affect model behavior. In this work, we study how instruction placement influences model performance in structured generation and show that merely changing the wording of schema keys, without modifying the prompt or model parameters, can significantly alter model performance under constrained decoding. Based on this observation, we propose to reinterpret structured generation as a multi-channel instruction problem, where instructions can be conveyed explicitly through prompts and implicitly through schema keys during decoding. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first work to systematically study how schema key formulation acts as an implicit instruction channel and affects model performance under constrained decoding. Experiments on multiple mathematical reasoning benchmarks show that different model families exhibit distinct sensitivities to these instruction channels: Qwen models consistently benefit from schema-level instructions, while LLaMA models rely more heavily on prompt-level guidance. We further observe non-additive interaction effects between instruction channels, showing that combining multiple channels does not always lead to further improvement. These findings suggest that schema design not only determines output structure, but also carries instruction signals, offering a new perspective on structured generation in LLMs.
2604.14862.paper_summary_zh: 受限解碼已被廣泛應用於大型語言模型（LLMs）的結構化生成，確保輸出滿足預定格式，如 JSON 和 XML。<lb/>然而，現有的方法主要將模式視為純粹的結構約束，忽略了它們的語言表述可能影響模型行為的可能性。<lb/>在本研究中，我們探討了指令位置如何影響結構化生成中的模型性能，並顯示僅僅改變模式鍵的措辭，而不修改提示或模型參數，可以顯著改變模型在受限解碼下的性能。<lb/>基於這一觀察，我們提出將結構化生成重新詮釋為多通道指令問題，其中指令可以通過提示明確地傳達，也可以在解碼過程中通過模式鍵隱含地傳達。<lb/>據我們所知，這是第一個系統性研究模式鍵表述如何作為隱性指令通道並影響受限解碼下模型性能的工作。<lb/>在多個數學推理基準上的實驗顯示，不同的模型家族對這些指令通道表現出明顯的敏感性：Qwen 模型始終受益於模式級指令，而 LLaMA 模型則更依賴於提示級指導。<lb/>我們進一步觀察到指令通道之間的非加性互動效應，顯示結合多個通道並不總是導致進一步的改善。<lb/>這些發現表明，模式設計不僅決定輸出結構，還攜帶指令信號，為 LLMs 中的結構化生成提供了一個新的視角。
2604.14862.author: Yifan Le et.al.
